mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, systematically supporting higher S&P 500 valuations over the medium term.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates exceeding 8.0% signal persistent weakness in consumer demand and labor market slack, which depresses corporate earnings and drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE > 8.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in high-unemployment regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized positive growth in both GDP and retail sales confirms a broad-based economic expansion, reducing recession risk and driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates increasing capacity utilization and operational efficiency, which boosts corporate profit margins and supports positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO is negative or near zero over any 3-year rolling window, or rising production coincides with falling equity prices due to supply-side cost shocks.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation readings above 225 trigger market fears of premature monetary tightening, leading to a compression of equity valuations and negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 225, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive payroll growth combined with falling unemployment confirms genuine labor market tightening, supporting wage growth and consumer spending, which drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect is statistically insignificant, or 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 700,000 units signal strong confidence in the real economy and future construction spending, acting as a leading indicator for positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for HOUST is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or high housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Weak GDP growth in a zero-rate environment triggers expectations of further central bank accommodation (QE), which boosts risk asset prices despite weak economic fundamentals.
failure_condition: The 'bad news is good news' dynamic reverses, with weak GDP and low rates leading to negative equity returns in > 50% of triggered instances.
